body.label: thm:cancel_c_mod_mc

hypothesis.assumption: c > 0 and ac is congruent to bc modulo mc.

body.claim: If ac is congruent to bc modulo mc and c > 0, then a is congruent to b modulo m.

proof.assumption: c > 0 and ac is congruent to bc modulo mc.

proof.proof_method: By the definition of congruence modulo mc applied to the assumption that ac is congruent to bc modulo mc.

proof.statement: There exists an integer k such that ac - bc = (a - b)c = kmc.

proof.proof_method: This follows from rewriting ac - bc as (a - b)c and using ac - bc = kmc.
Cancel the nonzero factor c from both sides of (a - b)c = (km)c.
Since a - b = km for some integer k, m divides a - b by definition of divisibility.

proof.claim: a is congruent to b modulo m.

results_used.statement: By definition of congruence modulo m, m divides a - b if and only if a is congruent to b modulo m.
